verdict_explanation: A clean immutable value type using Foundation.Decimal for money, but the core pricing arithmetic is copy-pasted across all three public methods. Fixable without new structure.

architecture_quality.proof: Sources/OrderCalculator.swift:20-58 — pricing computation re-derived in three methods; otherwise a clean single value type

state_management.proof: Sources/OrderCalculator.swift:17-18 — items/taxRate are let; no mutable runtime concern

concurrency.proof: Sources/OrderCalculator.swift — no Task/actor/async; synchronous value type, vacuously race-free

test_strategy.proof: Sources/OrderCalculator.swift:20-58 — public methods compile under swiftc -typecheck; no planted regression test in this fixture

credibility.proof: Sources/OrderCalculator.swift:5-9 — honest doc comment, but triplication is a real leak

data_flow.proof: Sources/OrderCalculator.swift:17-18 — explicit inputs/outputs, pure, no globals

framework_idioms.proof: Sources/OrderCalculator.swift:13,18,23 — Decimal money arithmetic, not Double

authority_map.concern: Order pricing computation (per-line total, subtotal, tax, grand total)
readers: checkout screen (caller of the three methods)

strengths: Money handled with Foundation.Decimal, not Double — avoids binary-float rounding error on currency (Sources/OrderCalculator.swift:13,18,23)

findings.title: Collapse the triplicated subtotal/tax computation into single owners
findings.why_it_matters: A single pricing-rule change (rounding, a discount, a second tax) must be edited in three methods, and the copies can silently drift into returning inconsistent totals to the checkout screen.
findings.what_is_wrong: The subtotal-accumulation loop is copy-pasted verbatim in subtotal(), totalWithTax(), and receiptLines(), and the tax step `sum * taxRate` is duplicated in totalWithTax() and receiptLines(). No single owner of the pricing arithmetic exists.
findings.test_failed: duplicated authority / weak domain model (not an abstraction-removal test)
findings.leverage_impact: Callers of the three methods cannot trust all totals were computed by the same rule; each method re-derives the arithmetic.
findings.locality_impact: A pricing change is non-local: it spreads across three methods instead of one helper.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Duplicated authority over the core pricing computation is a domain/simplicity defect; drift between the copies produces user-visible total mismatches that no structure prevents.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Reuse subtotal() inside totalWithTax() and receiptLines(); add one private tax(on:) helper for the `* taxRate` step; add a lineTotal computed property on LineItem. No new types/protocols/files; behavior-preserving.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: Collapsing the triplicated subtotal/tax computation into single owners removes duplicated authority over the pricing rule — one change site instead of three. Simplification (deletion of duplication), not deepening; no Module is removed.
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: Replace the hand-rolled accumulation loops with reduce; give LineItem a lineTotal property.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not introduce a PricingStrategy protocol, a Calculator interface, or separate Subtotal/Tax modules — a costume layer for pure synchronous arithmetic.
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: The consolidation is behavior-preserving and verified by swiftc -typecheck Sources/OrderCalculator.swift.

backlog.title: Collapse the triplicated subtotal/tax computation into single owners
backlog.why_it_matters: Removes duplicated authority over the core pricing rule, eliminating drift between the three totals.
backlog.score_impact: Code simplicity, Architecture quality, and Domain modeling each +1.5 to +2.0

narrative: The structure is sound where it exists, but the entry's one job — computing an order total — is authored three times. The honest repair is deleting duplication and giving the per-line total a home, not erecting a PricingStrategy Seam over pure arithmetic.
